content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 10, 1990

Ms. Puchi Carrion
Campaign Coordinator
Duplissea For California State
Board of Education
P.O. Box 1332
San Carlos, CA 94070

Re: Your Request For Advice
Our File No. A-90-624

Dear Ms. Carrion:

You have requested advice under the campaign provisions of
the Political Reform Act.1/

FACTS

Mr. Duplissea's campaign committee, Friends of Bill
Duplissea, I.D. No. 900923 ("the committee") paid for credit card
charges, some of which were for personal expenditures and some of
which were reimbursed by others. It is unclear for your letter
whether some of the charges were also paid by Mr. Duplissea's
other committee (I.D. No. 890411). The charges were made to Mr.
Duplissea's personal credit card, and were paid by the
committee(s) due to an oversight on the part of campaign staff.
Mr. Duplissea has made reimbursements to the committee in the
amount of $2,360.86 and the checks have been deposited in the
committee's account.

QUESTION

What is the proper method of reporting the personal or other
non-campaign credit card charges which were paid by the
committee(s)?

_________________________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Ms. Puchi Carrion
Page Two


CONCLUSION

     An amendment (Form 405) may be filed to each campaign
disclosure statement previously filed which reported payments made
(or accrued expenses) for the personal or non-campaign related
charges.  The amendment should itemize each reported charge
(either accrued expense or expenditure) which was for personal, or
other non-campaign related purposes, along with a notation that
the charges have been reimbursed by Mr. Duplissea's personal
checks.  If some of the payments in question were made by the old
Assembly committee (I.D. No. 890411), the amendments should
indicate that Mr. Duplissea's reimbursements will be made to the
Friends of Bill Duplissea, I.D. No. 900323.

     The reimbursement checks should be reported on Schedule G
(Miscellaneous Increases To Cash) of the committee's Form 490
covering the period during which they were received by the
committee.  An explanation should be made that the purpose of the
payments received is to reimburse the committee for personal and
non-campaign related items which were inadvertently charged to the
committee's credit card, or which were charged to Mr. Duplissea's
personal credit card and inadvertently paid by the committee,
whichever the case may be.

     If you have any questions about this letter, please call me
at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel
                                        [signature]
                                        By:  Jeanne Pritchard
                                             Chief, Technical Assistance
                                             and Analysis Division

What is the proper method of reporting the personal or other non-campaign credit card charges which were paid by the committee(s)?

1, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

An amendment (Form 405) may be filed to each campaign disclosure statement previously filed which reported payments made (or accrued expenses) for the personal or non-campaign related charges. The amendment should itemize each reported charge (either accrued expense or expenditure) which was for personal, or other non-campaign related purposes, along with a notation that the charges have been reimbursed by Mr. Duplissea's personal checks. If some of the payments in question were made by the old Assembly committee (I.D. No. 890411), the amendments should indicate that Mr. Duplissea's reimbursements will be made to the Friends of Bill Duplissea, I.D. No. 900323.

The reimbursement checks should be reported on Schedule G (Miscellaneous Increases To Cash) of the committee's Form 490 covering the period during which they were received by the committee. An explanation should be made that the purpose of the payments received is to reimburse the committee for personal and non-campaign related items which were inadvertently charged to the committee's credit card, or which were charged to Mr. Duplissea's personal credit card and inadvertently paid by the committee, whichever the case may be.
